data.title: Russian Foreign Minister: The Middle East crisis will not be resolved in the short term.
data.description: The Russian Foreign Minister stated that uranium enrichment is Iran's indisputable right. The Middle East crisis will not be resolved in the short term, and efforts to "resolve" the crisis will be fruitless. (Solid Intel)

data.title: Ethereum's Weekly MACD Indicates Potential Bullish Momentum
data.description:
On April 15, Ethereum's weekly MACD is reportedly forming a golden cross pattern, according to BlockBeats. Analyst Alicharts notes that in the past three instances of Ethereum's weekly MACD golden cross, ETH prices increased by 130%, 74%, and 98% respectively.

It is important to highlight that while a MACD golden cross is often seen as a signal of strengthening momentum, historical performance does not guarantee future outcomes. A comprehensive analysis should consider the macroeconomic environment and overall market sentiment.

data.title: Citrea's Technical Milestone: Focus Shifts to User Acquisition
data.description: Citrea has successfully demonstrated its technical prowess, according to Tiger Research. The Bitcoin Layer 2 project has validated its zero-knowledge technology by launching a live mainnet that utilizes BitVM for direct verification by the Bitcoin mainnet. According to NS3.AI, the next step for Citrea is to concentrate on expanding its user base.

data.title: Hackers used the Obsidian plugin to launch the PHANTOMPULSE Trojan.
data.description: According to Elastic Security Labs, threat actors impersonated venture capital firms, luring targets to open Obsidian notes containing malicious code via LinkedIn and Telegram. This attack exploited Obsidian's Shell Commands plugin, executing the malicious payload without exploiting vulnerabilities when the victim opened the notes library. The attack discovered PHANTOMPULSE, a previously undocumented Windows Remote Access Trojan (RAT), enabled blockchain C2 communication via Ethereum transaction data. The macOS payload used an obfuscated AppleScript delivery tool and a Telegram channel as a backup C2. Elastic Defend detected and blocked the attack before PHANTOMPULSE could execute.

data.title: Societe Generale-FORGE Expands USD CoinVertible Access via MetaMask Partnership
data.description: Societe Generale-FORGE has partnered with Consensys to make its USD CoinVertible stablecoin accessible to millions of MetaMask users. According to NS3.AI, this collaboration allows the bank's dollar-denominated token to be integrated into self-custodial crypto wallets. Societe Generale-FORGE, the digital asset division of Societe Generale SA, aims to enhance the reach and usability of its stablecoin through this strategic move.

data.title: First Digital Partners with Singapore Gulf Bank to Enhance FDUSD Infrastructure
data.description: First Digital has announced a partnership with Singapore Gulf Bank to strengthen the institutional banking infrastructure for FDUSD. According to Foresight News, the collaboration aims to achieve near real-time settlement, optimize liquidity management, and reduce operational friction through SGB's dedicated stablecoin banking layer.

data.title: New Social Engineering Attack Targets Crypto and Finance Users
data.description: A new social engineering attack has been identified, targeting users in the cryptocurrency and finance sectors. According to PANews, Elastic Security Labs discovered that attackers are exploiting the community plugin feature of the note-taking application Obsidian to distribute malware capable of controlling victims' devices. The attackers employ sophisticated social engineering tactics via LinkedIn and Telegram, posing as venture capital firms. They create a business context centered around financial services, particularly cryptocurrency liquidity solutions, to lure victims into opening a cloud-hosted library controlled by the attackers in Obsidian and enabling community plugin synchronization. The malicious plugin then silently executes the attack chain.

data.title: Zerion disclosed that it was attacked by North Korean hackers using AI for social engineering, resulting in the theft of approximately $100,000.
data.description: Crypto wallet Zerion disclosed that North Korean hackers used AI in a long-term social engineering attack, stealing approximately $100,000 from the company's hot wallet last week. Zerion confirmed that user funds, applications, and infrastructure were unaffected and has proactively disabled its web application as a precaution. Zerion also stated that the attackers obtained login sessions and credentials of some team members, as well as the company's hot wallet private key, noting that AI is changing the way cyber threats operate. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: SIX Group to Offer Onchain Stock Market Data via Chainlink Nodes
data.description: European stock exchange operator SIX Group is set to provide stock market data onchain through Chainlink nodes. According to NS3.AI, this integration aims to cater to the tokenized asset market by enabling blockchain developers to create financial products using institutional-grade data.

data.title: Hanwha Investment & Securities Advances Digital Asset Strategy
data.description: Hanwha Investment & Securities is taking steps to specialize in digital assets. According to NS3.AI, the company has proposed a 'Hanwha TradFi' infrastructure and is actively recruiting Web3 professionals to form a dedicated team.

data.title: Ripple CEO Highlights Progress in U.S. Crypto Regulation
data.description: Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse recently celebrated his 11th anniversary at the company by noting advancements in U.S. cryptocurrency regulation. According to NS3.AI, Garlinghouse highlighted that XRP has been officially recognized as a commodity rather than a security by U.S. regulators. Despite this progress, he emphasized the need for lawmakers to enact a comprehensive crypto market structure bill to further clarify and support the industry.

data.title: BitMine Reports Significant Revenue Increase Amid ETH Staking Strategy
data.description:
BitMine has submitted its quarterly 10-Q financial report to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) for the period ending February 28, 2026. According to Foresight News, the report reveals a substantial increase in revenue, rising from $1.52 million in the same period last year to $11.04 million, largely driven by ETH staking income totaling $10.2 million. However, the company faced an unrealized loss of $3.78 billion due to the decline in ETH prices, resulting in a net loss of $3.82 billion for the quarter.

As of the end of the reporting period, BitMine's total assets amounted to $9.89 billion, with digital assets comprising $8.81 billion. This includes holdings of 4,473,459 ETH and 195 BTC.

In its latest announcement dated April 12, BitMine disclosed an increase in its ETH holdings to 4,874,858, marking an addition of approximately 400,000 ETH since the end of February. The company continues to implement its 'Alchemy of 5%' strategy for accumulating ETH.

data.title: ETH/BTC Exchange Rate Reaches Two-Month High
data.description:
The ETH/BTC exchange rate has recently surged, reaching a near 70-day high by surpassing 0.032 USD. According to BlockBeats, this marks the highest point in over two months.

As a result, a hedge trader known as BobbyBigSize on Hyperliquid has seen significant impacts on their positions. The trader's main positions, consisting of an ETH short and a BTC long, have a combined value of $34.6 million, with unrealized losses now totaling approximately $1.78 million.

Additionally, the trader holds a $12.45 million short position in HYPE, with an unrealized loss of $1.4 million, along with a $3.8 million long position in SOL and other hedge positions.

Address: 0x7fdafde5cfb5465924316eced2d3715494c517d1

data.title: Ethereum Treasury Firm BMNR Reports $3.82 Billion Net Loss This Quarter
data.description: Ethereum treasury firm BMNR has reported a net loss of $3.82 billion for the current quarter. According to Odaily, the financial results highlight significant challenges faced by the company amid fluctuating market conditions. BMNR's performance reflects broader trends within the cryptocurrency sector, where volatility continues to impact financial outcomes. The report underscores the ongoing difficulties in navigating the complex landscape of digital assets.

data.title: Gate releases its latest transparency report, highlighting the continued empowerment of the trading ecosystem with AI.
data.description: Digital asset trading platform Gate released its March 2026 Transparency Report. Around its Intelligent Web3 strategy, the platform is accelerating the construction of an AI product matrix centered on Gate for AI, GateClaw, GateAI, and GateRouter, and promoting the deep integration of AI technology into the trading ecosystem. Gate AI has covered more than 80 application scenarios, encompassing market analysis, strategy assistance, and investment research support, and is gradually penetrating into high-frequency trading and investment research, becoming a key driver of platform capability upgrades. In terms of business data, the Gate TradFi sector saw a peak daily trading volume exceeding $20 billion, covering more than 350 assets, with 174 new assets added in the month, becoming a core growth engine. According to CoinDesk data, the platform's user base has exceeded 51 million, and its derivatives market share rose to a record high of 12.2%; its spot trading volume remains the second largest globally, its liquidity consistently ranks among the top three, and both trading depth and activity are improving simultaneously. Gate's institutional business also performed exceptionally well, earning it the title of "Best Institutional Trading Platform" on the BeInCrypto rankings. Leveraging its approximately 2ms low-latency matching, deep liquidity, and multi-asset system, combined with SuperLink cross-market accounts and third-party custody partnerships, the platform further enhances capital efficiency and security. Gate is continuously strengthening its leading position in the global trading and institutional services sector through the synergistic upgrade of its AI product matrix and multi-asset trading capabilities.

data.title: Bitcoin developers have proposed BIP-361 to counter potential future quantum attack risks.
data.description: Jameson Loop, a Bitcoin contributor, and other cryptographers have proposed an initiative that could force Bitcoin holders to migrate their tokens to new quantum-resistant addresses, or have their tokens permanently frozen by the network itself. In this scenario, holders would technically still "own" the coins but would lose the ability to transfer them. This is known as Bitcoin Improvement Proposal BIP-361 and was updated in Bitcoin's official proposal repository on Tuesday under the title "Post-Quantum Migration with Old Signature Elimination." BIP-361 builds on BIP-360, a proposal put forward in February. BIP-360 introduced a soft fork (a network upgrade) designed to enable a new transaction type called "Payments to Merklegen" (P2MR). This approach borrows from Bitcoin's Taproot (P2TR) framework but removes the key-based spending path, thus removing an element widely considered to pose a quantum-age risk. The BIP-361 proposal divides the migration into three phases. Phase A, which begins three years after activation, prohibits anyone from sending new Bitcoins to old, quantum-vulnerable addresses. You can still spend from these addresses, but you cannot receive any coins. Phase B, launched five years after activation, will completely disable old-style signatures (ECDSA and Schnorr), and the network will reject any attempt to spend coins from wallets vulnerable to quantum attacks. Essentially, your coins will be frozen. Finally, there is Phase C, a rescue scheme still under research: it is possible for the holder of the frozen wallet to prove ownership through zero-knowledge proofs (a method of proving knowledge of a secret without revealing the secret itself). If successful, the coins frozen in Phase B can be recovered. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Hermès Reports First Quarter Revenue Below Expectations
data.description: Hermès reported a total revenue of 4.07 billion euros for the first quarter. According to Jin10, this figure fell short of institutional expectations, which were set at 4.16 billion euros.

data.title: TRON Initiates Quantum-Resistant Upgrade Plan
data.description:
TRON has officially launched its quantum-resistant upgrade plan, according to Foresight News. The initiative involves deploying a post-quantum signature scheme on the mainnet that complies with the standards set by the U.S. National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST). The technical roadmap for this upgrade will be released soon.

TRON's founder, Justin Sun, emphasized that quantum security should not be a matter of debate but a fundamental feature. He assured that TRON is committed to safeguarding user assets from potential quantum threats.

data.title: Antofagasta Reports First Quarter Gold Production
data.description: Antofagasta, a mining company, reported that its gold production for the first quarter reached 46,500 ounces. According to Jin10, this production figure reflects the company's ongoing efforts to maintain its output levels. The report highlights Antofagasta's commitment to sustaining its production targets amid fluctuating market conditions. The company continues to focus on optimizing its operations to achieve consistent results in the mining sector.

data.title: CryptoValley in Switzerland raised $728 million through 31 deals in 2025, a 37% year-over-year increase.
data.description: According to CV VC's annual report, Switzerland's Crypto Valley raised $728 million in 31 deals in 2025, a 37% year-on-year increase, accounting for 47% of total European blockchain venture capital investment. The report states that global blockchain venture capital investment grew by 30% to $15.5 billion, with 986 deals. One deal played a key role: The Open Network led the way with a $400 million funding round, followed by Sygnum Bank ($58 million), M0 ($40 million), Impossible Cloud Network ($34 million), and CratD2C ($30 million). Blockchain networks attracted 62% of the funding, infrastructure accounted for 14%, and centralized financial services and DeFi applications each accounted for 10%. Crypto Valley currently boasts 1,766 active blockchain companies, a 134% increase since 2020. Despite increased funding, the valuation of the top 50 crypto projects fell to $467 billion, and the number of unicorns dropped from 17 to 10. This was mainly due to the weak market at the end of the year, which caused the valuations of six token projects to fall below the $1 billion threshold, and the departure of 21Shares from the ecosystem after its acquisition by FalconX.
